# Global Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent running in a headless orchestration session.
There is no human in the loop — do not ask questions or wait for input.

## Ground rules

1. Read and follow the project's CLAUDE.md for coding conventions and standards.
2. Never use interactive commands, slash commands, or plan mode.
3. Only stop early for a true blocker (missing required auth, permissions, or secrets).
   If blocked, post the blocker details as a Linear comment and stop.
4. Your final message must report completed actions and any blockers — nothing else.

## Execution approach

- Spend extra effort on planning and verification.
- Read all relevant files before writing code.
- When planning: read CLAUDE.md, the existing code in the area you are modifying, and any related docs.
- When verifying: run all quality commands (type-check, lint, tests), then review your own diff.
- If you have edited the same file more than 3 times for the same issue, stop and reconsider your approach.

## Session startup

Before starting any implementation work:

1. Run the project's type-check command to verify the codebase compiles clean.
2. Run the project's test command to verify all tests pass.
3. If either fails, investigate and fix before starting new work.

## Linear workpad

Use a single Linear comment as a persistent workpad:

- Title: `## Workpad`
- Update it at each milestone with: current status, decisions made, and next steps.
- On rework runs, append the rework section — do not delete prior content.

## Rework awareness

Every prompt in this workflow serves both first-run and rework cases.
On rework runs, the workspace already contains prior work.  Check for:

- An existing feature branch (do not create a new one)
- An open PR (push to it, do not open a second)
- Review comments requesting changes (address them specifically)
- Prior workpad content (append to it, do not overwrite)

Understand the problem thoroughly before any code is written.  Your output is
an investigation summary posted as a Linear comment — not code changes.

2. Identify the relevant source files — read them, understand the architecture.
3. If the issue is a bug: reproduce it first (run the failing test or repro steps).
4. If the issue is a feature: map out which files/modules need changes.

6. Post the summary as a Linear comment titled `## Investigation`.

Perfect! I have gathered comprehensive information. Let me create a final summary document for you:

## Comprehensive CRM Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. CRM Crate Structure

**Location**: `/Users/samjp/Code/stokowski-workspaces/CHA-11/crates/crm/`

**Files**:
- `src/types.rs` - Domain types (27.7 KB)
- `src/store.rs` - Trait definition (4.1 KB)
- `src/store_sqlite.rs` - SQLite implementation (26.6 KB)
- `src/store_memory.rs` - In-memory implementation for tests (16.5 KB)
- `src/error.rs` - Error types (1.2 KB)
- `src/lib.rs` - Public API and migrations (1.6 KB)
- `Cargo.toml` - Package configuration
- `migrations/20260317000000_init.sql` - Initial schema (3.6 KB)
- `migrations/20260317000001_crm_types.sql` - Type enrichment (2.7 KB)

### 2. CRM Domain Types (Already Defined)

**Enums** (all derive `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize`):

- **`ContactStage`** - Contact lifecycle: `Lead` (default), `Prospect`, `Active`, `Inactive`, `Closed`
- **`MatterStatus`** - Matter state: `Open` (default), `OnHold`, `Closed`, `Archived`
- **`MatterPhase`** - Matter progress: `Intake` (default), `Discovery`, `Negotiation`, `Resolution`, `Review`, `Closed`
- **`PracticeArea`** - Legal domain: `Corporate`, `Employment`, `FamilyLaw`, `Immigration`, `IntellectualProperty`, `Litigation`, `RealEstate`, `Tax`, `Other` (default)
- **`InteractionKind`** - Interaction type: `Call`, `Email`, `Message`, `Meeting`, `Note`, `Document`

All enums implement:
- `as_str()` → `&'static str` (never allocated)
- `std::fmt::Display` (displays as lowercase snake_case)
- `std::str::FromStr` (parses from string with error on unknown value)

**Core Structs**:

1. **`Contact`** - Person or entity interacting with the system
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `name: String` (display name)
   - `source: Option<String>` (origin channel: "telegram", "whatsapp", "csv-import", etc.)
   - `external_id: Option<String>` (channel-native ID)
   - `email: Option<Secret<String>>` (PII, wrapped in `secrecy::Secret`)
   - `phone: Option<Secret<String>>` (PII, wrapped in `secrecy::Secret`)
   - `stage: ContactStage`
   - `metadata: serde_json::Value` (arbitrary JSON object)
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds since epoch)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds since epoch)
   - Custom `Debug` impl redacts PII fields as `[REDACTED]`
   - Custom `PartialEq` compares `Secret<String>` fields by exposing and comparing values
   - Constructors: `new(name)` and `with_source(name, source, external_id)`

2. **`ContactChannel`** - Channel identity linking contact to external platform
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `contact_id: String` (foreign key to contact)
   - `channel_type: String` (e.g., "telegram", "slack", "email")
   - `channel_id: String` (channel-native identifier)
   - `display_name: Option<String>` (e.g., username, email)
   - `verified: bool` (verification status)
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - Constructor: `new(contact_id, channel_type, channel_id)`

3. **`Matter`** - Legal case or work item
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `contact_id: Option<String>` (nullable: matter may span multiple contacts or exist independently)
   - `title: String` (short description)
   - `description: Option<String>` (longer description)
   - `status: MatterStatus`
   - `phase: MatterPhase`
   - `practice_area: PracticeArea`
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - Constructor: `new(contact_id, title, practice_area)`

4. **`Interaction`** - Recorded touchpoint between system and contact
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `contact_id: String` (required foreign key)
   - `matter_id: Option<String>` (optional foreign key to matter)
   - `kind: InteractionKind`
   - `summary: String` (what occurred)
   - `channel: Option<String>` (where it happened: "telegram", etc.)
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - Constructor: `new(contact_id, kind, summary)`

### 3. Store Trait Pattern

**File**: `src/store.rs`

The `CrmStore` trait uses `#[async_trait]` and defines CRUD operations:

```
#[async_trait]
pub trait CrmStore: Send + Sync {
    // Contacts: list, get, get_by_external, upsert, delete
    // ContactChannels: list_channels_for_contact, get_channel_by_external, upsert_channel, delete_channel
    // Matters: list_matters, list_matters_by_contact, get_matter, upsert_matter, delete_matter
    // Interactions: list_interactions_by_contact, list_interactions_by_matter, get_interaction, upsert_interaction, delete_interaction
}
```

All operations are idempotent (delete on non-existent returns `Ok(())`).

### 4. SQLite Implementation Patterns

**File**: `src/store_sqlite.rs`

**Key Patterns**:

1. **Row Types**: Define `struct *Row` with `#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]` for each entity
   - Stores enums as `String` (e.g., `stage: String`)
   - Stores `bool` as `i64` (SQLite has no native bool)
   - Stores `Secret<String>` as `Option<String>` in schema
   - Stores timestamps as `i64`

2. **Conversion**: `TryFrom<*Row>` for entities that parse enums
   - Uses `Error::parse()` for unknown enum values
   - Uses `Secret::new()` to wrap PII fields on deserialization
   - Uses `serde_json::from_str()` with fallback to `"{}"` for metadata

3. **Query Pattern**:
   ```rust
   sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>("SELECT ... FROM crm_contacts WHERE ...")
       .bind(param1)
       .bind(param2)
       .fetch_optional(&self.pool)  // or .fetch_all()
       .await?
   ```

4. **Upsert Pattern**: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET ...`
   - No created_at update on conflict (preserves original creation time)
   - Uses `contact.stage.as_str()` to convert enum to string
   - Uses `s.expose_secret().clone()` for `Secret<String>` fields
   - Converts bool to `i64` with `as i64`

5. **Constructor**: Takes `SqlitePool` by value
   ```rust
   pub fn new(pool: SqlitePool) -> Self {
       Self { pool }
   }
   ```

### 5. In-Memory Implementation Pattern

**File**: `src/store_memory.rs`

- Uses `RwLock<HashMap<String, T>>` for each entity type
- Implements same `CrmStore` trait
- Manually sorts by `updated_at DESC` when returning lists
- Uses `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` for poisoned locks
- Designed for testing and development

### 6. Error Handling

**File**: `src/error.rs`

```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum Error {
    #[error(transparent)]
    Sqlx(#[from] sqlx::Error),
    #[error(transparent)]
    Migration(#[from] sqlx::migrate::MigrateError),
    #[error(transparent)]
    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),
    #[error("record not found: {id}")]
    NotFound { id: String },
    #[error("invalid value for {field}: {value:?}")]
    Parse { field: &'static str, value: String },
    #[error("{context}: {source}")]
    External { context: String, #[source] source: Box<dyn StdError + Send + Sync> },
}

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, Error>;
```

Helper constructors:
- `Error::not_found(id)` - Creates `NotFound` variant
- `Error::parse(field, value)` - Creates `Parse` variant (for enum conversion failures)
- `Error::external(context, source)` - Creates `External` variant

### 7. Database Schema

**Migration 1** (`20260317000000_init.sql`):
- `crm_contacts` table (id, name, source, external_id, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
- `crm_matters` table (id, title, status, contact_id FK, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
- `crm_interactions` table (id, contact_id FK, matter_id FK, kind, body, metadata, created_at)
- `crm_contact_channels` table (id, contact_id FK, channel_type, channel_identifier, verified, created_at)
- Indexes on: `updated_at DESC`, `source+external_id`, `contact_id`, `status`, `created_at DESC`
- Foreign key constraints: `ON DELETE SET NULL` for matters.contact_id, `ON DELETE CASCADE` for interactions/channels

**Migration 2** (`20260317000001_crm_types.sql`):
- Adds `email`, `phone`, `stage` to contacts
- Renames `channel_identifier` → `channel_id` in contact_channels
- Adds `display_name`, `updated_at` to contact_channels
- Adds `description`, `phase`, `practice_area` to matters
- Renames `body` → `summary` in interactions
- Adds `channel`, `updated_at` to interactions
- Rebuilds indexes after schema changes

### 8. Cargo Configuration

**File**: `crates/crm/Cargo.toml`

```toml
[package]
edition.workspace = true
name              = "moltis-crm"
version.workspace = true

[dependencies]
async-trait    = { workspace = true }
moltis-metrics = { optional = true, workspace = true }
secrecy        = { workspace = true }
serde          = { workspace = true }
serde_json     = { workspace = true }
sqlx           = { workspace = true }
thiserror      = { workspace = true }
tokio          = { workspace = true }
tracing        = { workspace = true }
uuid           = { workspace = true }

[dev-dependencies]
tempfile = { workspace = true }
tokio    = { features = ["rt", "macros"], workspace = true }

[features]
default = []
metrics = ["dep:moltis-metrics"]

[lints]
workspace = true
```

**Key Points**:
- No `metrics` or `tracing` features enabled by default (unlike sessions which enables metrics)
- Uses workspace dependencies exclusively (no hardcoded versions)
- `tempfile` for test support
- Opt-in metrics feature with `moltis-metrics`

### 9. Workspace Integration

**Root Workspace**:
- CRM is in `default-members` in root `Cargo.toml`
- CRM is in workspace dependencies: `moltis-crm = { path = "crates/crm" }`

**Gateway Integration** (`crates/gateway/Cargo.toml`):
- Dependency: `moltis-crm = { optional = true, workspace = true }`
- Feature: `crm = ["dep:moltis-crm"]`
- Default feature includes `crm`

**CLI Integration** (`crates/cli/Cargo.toml`):
- Dependency: `moltis-crm` (direct dependency)
- Feature: `crm = ["dep:moltis-crm", "moltis-gateway/crm"]`
- Default feature includes `crm`

**Server Initialization** (`crates/gateway/src/server.rs`):
```rust
// Line ~1874
#[cfg(feature = "crm")]
moltis_crm::run_migrations(&db_pool)
    .await
    .expect("failed to run crm migrations");
```

Migration order in server.rs (lines 1852-1874):
1. projects
2. sessions (after projects due to FK)
3. cron
4. gateway
5. vault
6. **crm** (last)

### 10. Public API (via lib.rs)

```rust
pub use {
    error::{Error, Result},
    store::{CrmStore, MemoryCrmStore, SqliteCrmStore},
    types::{
        Contact, ContactChannel, ContactStage, Interaction, InteractionKind, 
        Matter, MatterPhase, MatterStatus, PracticeArea,
    },
};

pub async fn run_migrations(pool: &sqlx::SqlitePool) -> Result<()> {
    sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")
        .set_ignore_missing(true)
        .run(pool)
        .await?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### 11. Testing Patterns

**SQLite Tests**:
- Setup: Create in-memory SQLite pool with `:memory:` URL
- Run migrations before each test: `crate::run_migrations(&pool).await.unwrap()`
- Initialize store: `SqliteCrmStore::new(pool)`
- Use explicit `.unwrap()` (allowed in tests via `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]`)

**Memory Tests**:
- Setup: `MemoryCrmStore::new()`
- No migrations needed
- Same test structure as SQLite

**Test Coverage**:
- CRUD operations (create, read, update via upsert, delete)
- External ID lookups
- Idempotent deletes
- Roundtrip serialization/deserialization
- PII field handling (encryption/redaction)
- Enum parsing and conversion
- Foreign key relationships (matter linked to contact, interaction linked to matter)

### 12. Naming Conventions

**Tables**: `crm_contacts`, `crm_matters`, `crm_interactions`, `crm_contact_channels`

**Indexes**: `idx_crm_contacts_updated_at`, `idx_crm_contact_channels_contact_id`, etc.

**Functions**: 
- List all: `list()`, `list_matters()`, `list_interactions_by_contact()`
- Get single: `get()`, `get_matter()`, `get_interaction()`
- Lookup by external: `get_by_external()`, `get_channel_by_external()`
- Write: `upsert()`, `upsert_matter()`, `upsert_channel()`
- Delete: `delete()`, `delete_matter()` (idempotent)

**Error Context**:
- Enum parsing: `Error::parse("contact.stage", value)` includes dot-separated path

### 13. Key Invariants & Patterns

1. **IDs are UUIDs v4** generated via `uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()`
2. **Timestamps are milliseconds** since Unix epoch, stored as `u64` in code and `i64` in SQLite
3. **PII Protection**: Email and phone wrapped in `secrecy::Secret<String>`, exposed only at consumption points
4. **Enums stored as strings** in database, parsed with proper error context on retrieval
5. **Metadata is JSON** (arbitrary structure), serialized/deserialized as needed
6. **Foreign keys preserve referential integrity**: matters.contact_id uses `ON DELETE SET NULL`, interactions use `ON DELETE CASCADE`
7. **Unique constraints**: channel_type + channel_id must be unique across all contacts
8. **Ordering**: All list operations return results sorted by `updated_at DESC` (most recent first)
9. **Idempotent operations**: All delete operations succeed even if record doesn't exist
10. **No manual lock management**: Mutex only guards state, never used as a synchronization primitive (`Mutex<()>` forbidden)

### 14. Comparison with Other Store Implementations

| Aspect | Projects | Sessions | Cron | CRM |
|--------|----------|----------|------|-----|
| **Trait** | `ProjectStore` | `SessionStore` | `CronStore` | `CrmStore` |
| **SQLite Impl** | `SqliteProjectStore` | File-based | `SqliteStore` | `SqliteCrmStore` |
| **Memory Impl** | `TomlProjectStore` | File-based | `MemoryStore` | `MemoryCrmStore` |
| **Feature** | Always enabled | Always enabled | Optional | Optional (default enabled) |
| **Migrations** | Single file (609B) | Multiple files | Multiple files | Two files (6.3 KB total) |
| **Default Metrics** | No | Yes | No | No |
| **Error Type** | `thiserror::Error` | Custom | `anyhow::Result` | `thiserror::Error` |

### 2. CRM Domain Types (Already Defined)

**Enums** (all derive `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize`):

- **`ContactStage`** - Contact lifecycle: `Lead` (default), `Prospect`, `Active`, `Inactive`, `Closed`
- **`MatterStatus`** - Matter state: `Open` (default), `OnHold`, `Closed`, `Archived`
- **`MatterPhase`** - Matter progress: `Intake` (default), `Discovery`, `Negotiation`, `Resolution`, `Review`, `Closed`
- **`PracticeArea`** - Legal domain: `Corporate`, `Employment`, `FamilyLaw`, `Immigration`, `IntellectualProperty`, `Litigation`, `RealEstate`, `Tax`, `Other` (default)
- **`InteractionKind`** - Interaction type: `Call`, `Email`, `Message`, `Meeting`, `Note`, `Document`

All enums implement:
- `as_str()` → `&'static str` (never allocated)
- `std::fmt::Display` (displays as lowercase snake_case)
- `std::str::FromStr` (parses from string with error on unknown value)

**Core Structs**:

1. **`Contact`** - Person or entity interacting with the system
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `name: String` (display name)
   - `source: Option<String>` (origin channel: "telegram", "whatsapp", "csv-import", etc.)
   - `external_id: Option<String>` (channel-native ID)
   - `email: Option<Secret<String>>` (PII, wrapped in `secrecy::Secret`)
   - `phone: Option<Secret<String>>` (PII, wrapped in `secrecy::Secret`)
   - `stage: ContactStage`
   - `metadata: serde_json::Value` (arbitrary JSON object)
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds since epoch)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds since epoch)
   - Custom `Debug` impl redacts PII fields as `[REDACTED]`
   - Custom `PartialEq` compares `Secret<String>` fields by exposing and comparing values
   - Constructors: `new(name)` and `with_source(name, source, external_id)`

2. **`ContactChannel`** - Channel identity linking contact to external platform
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `contact_id: String` (foreign key to contact)
   - `channel_type: String` (e.g., "telegram", "slack", "email")
   - `channel_id: String` (channel-native identifier)
   - `display_name: Option<String>` (e.g., username, email)
   - `verified: bool` (verification status)
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - Constructor: `new(contact_id, channel_type, channel_id)`

3. **`Matter`** - Legal case or work item
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `contact_id: Option<String>` (nullable: matter may span multiple contacts or exist independently)
   - `title: String` (short description)
   - `description: Option<String>` (longer description)
   - `status: MatterStatus`
   - `phase: MatterPhase`
   - `practice_area: PracticeArea`
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - Constructor: `new(contact_id, title, practice_area)`

4. **`Interaction`** - Recorded touchpoint between system and contact
   - `id: String` (UUID v4)
   - `contact_id: String` (required foreign key)
   - `matter_id: Option<String>` (optional foreign key to matter)
   - `kind: InteractionKind`
   - `summary: String` (what occurred)
   - `channel: Option<String>` (where it happened: "telegram", etc.)
   - `created_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - `updated_at: u64` (milliseconds)
   - Constructor: `new(contact_id, kind, summary)`

### 3. Store Trait Pattern

**File**: `src/store.rs`

The `CrmStore` trait uses `#[async_trait]` and defines CRUD operations:

```
#[async_trait]
pub trait CrmStore: Send + Sync {
    // Contacts: list, get, get_by_external, upsert, delete
    // ContactChannels: list_channels_for_contact, get_channel_by_external, upsert_channel, delete_channel
    // Matters: list_matters, list_matters_by_contact, get_matter, upsert_matter, delete_matter
    // Interactions: list_interactions_by_contact, list_interactions_by_matter, get_interaction, upsert_interaction, delete_interaction
}
```

All operations are idempotent (delete on non-existent returns `Ok(())`).

### 4. SQLite Implementation Patterns

**File**: `src/store_sqlite.rs`

**Key Patterns**:

1. **Row Types**: Define `struct *Row` with `#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]` for each entity
   - Stores enums as `String` (e.g., `stage: String`)
   - Stores `bool` as `i64` (SQLite has no native bool)
   - Stores `Secret<String>` as `Option<String>` in schema
   - Stores timestamps as `i64`

2. **Conversion**: `TryFrom<*Row>` for entities that parse enums
   - Uses `Error::parse()` for unknown enum values
   - Uses `Secret::new()` to wrap PII fields on deserialization
   - Uses `serde_json::from_str()` with fallback to `"{}"` for metadata

3. **Query Pattern**:
   ```rust
   sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>("SELECT ... FROM crm_contacts WHERE ...")
       .bind(param1)
       .bind(param2)
       .fetch_optional(&self.pool)  // or .fetch_all()
       .await?
   ```

4. **Upsert Pattern**: `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET ...`
   - No created_at update on conflict (preserves original creation time)
   - Uses `contact.stage.as_str()` to convert enum to string
   - Uses `s.expose_secret().clone()` for `Secret<String>` fields
   - Converts bool to `i64` with `as i64`

5. **Constructor**: Takes `SqlitePool` by value
   ```rust
   pub fn new(pool: SqlitePool) -> Self {
       Self { pool }
   }
   ```

### 5. In-Memory Implementation Pattern

**File**: `src/store_memory.rs`

- Uses `RwLock<HashMap<String, T>>` for each entity type
- Implements same `CrmStore` trait
- Manually sorts by `updated_at DESC` when returning lists
- Uses `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` for poisoned locks
- Designed for testing and development

### 6. Error Handling

**File**: `src/error.rs`

```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum Error {
    #[error(transparent)]
    Sqlx(#[from] sqlx::Error),
    #[error(transparent)]
    Migration(#[from] sqlx::migrate::MigrateError),
    #[error(transparent)]
    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),
    #[error("record not found: {id}")]
    NotFound { id: String },
    #[error("invalid value for {field}: {value:?}")]
    Parse { field: &'static str, value: String },
    #[error("{context}: {source}")]
    External { context: String, #[source] source: Box<dyn StdError + Send + Sync> },
}

pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, Error>;
```

Helper constructors:
- `Error::not_found(id)` - Creates `NotFound` variant
- `Error::parse(field, value)` - Creates `Parse` variant (for enum conversion failures)
- `Error::external(context, source)` - Creates `External` variant

### 7. Database Schema

**Migration 1** (`20260317000000_init.sql`):
- `crm_contacts` table (id, name, source, external_id, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
- `crm_matters` table (id, title, status, contact_id FK, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
- `crm_interactions` table (id, contact_id FK, matter_id FK, kind, body, metadata, created_at)
- `crm_contact_channels` table (id, contact_id FK, channel_type, channel_identifier, verified, created_at)
- Indexes on: `updated_at DESC`, `source+external_id`, `contact_id`, `status`, `created_at DESC`
- Foreign key constraints: `ON DELETE SET NULL` for matters.contact_id, `ON DELETE CASCADE` for interactions/channels

**Migration 2** (`20260317000001_crm_types.sql`):
- Adds `email`, `phone`, `stage` to contacts
- Renames `channel_identifier` → `channel_id` in contact_channels
- Adds `display_name`, `updated_at` to contact_channels
- Adds `description`, `phase`, `practice_area` to matters
- Renames `body` → `summary` in interactions
- Adds `channel`, `updated_at` to interactions
- Rebuilds indexes after schema changes

### 8. Cargo Configuration

**File**: `crates/crm/Cargo.toml`

```toml
[package]
edition.workspace = true
name              = "moltis-crm"
version.workspace = true

[dependencies]
async-trait    = { workspace = true }
moltis-metrics = { optional = true, workspace = true }
secrecy        = { workspace = true }
serde          = { workspace = true }
serde_json     = { workspace = true }
sqlx           = { workspace = true }
thiserror      = { workspace = true }
tokio          = { workspace = true }
tracing        = { workspace = true }
uuid           = { workspace = true }

[dev-dependencies]
tempfile = { workspace = true }
tokio    = { features = ["rt", "macros"], workspace = true }

[features]
default = []
metrics = ["dep:moltis-metrics"]

[lints]
workspace = true
```

**Key Points**:
- No `metrics` or `tracing` features enabled by default (unlike sessions which enables metrics)
- Uses workspace dependencies exclusively (no hardcoded versions)
- `tempfile` for test support
- Opt-in metrics feature with `moltis-metrics`

### 9. Workspace Integration

**Root Workspace**:
- CRM is in `default-members` in root `Cargo.toml`
- CRM is in workspace dependencies: `moltis-crm = { path = "crates/crm" }`

**Gateway Integration** (`crates/gateway/Cargo.toml`):
- Dependency: `moltis-crm = { optional = true, workspace = true }`
- Feature: `crm = ["dep:moltis-crm"]`
- Default feature includes `crm`

**CLI Integration** (`crates/cli/Cargo.toml`):
- Dependency: `moltis-crm` (direct dependency)
- Feature: `crm = ["dep:moltis-crm", "moltis-gateway/crm"]`
- Default feature includes `crm`

**Server Initialization** (`crates/gateway/src/server.rs`):
```rust
// Line ~1874
#[cfg(feature = "crm")]
moltis_crm::run_migrations(&db_pool)
    .await
    .expect("failed to run crm migrations");
```

Migration order in server.rs (lines 1852-1874):
1. projects
2. sessions (after projects due to FK)
3. cron
4. gateway
5. vault
6. **crm** (last)

### 10. Public API (via lib.rs)

```rust
pub use {
    error::{Error, Result},
    store::{CrmStore, MemoryCrmStore, SqliteCrmStore},
    types::{
        Contact, ContactChannel, ContactStage, Interaction, InteractionKind, 
        Matter, MatterPhase, MatterStatus, PracticeArea,
    },
};

pub async fn run_migrations(pool: &sqlx::SqlitePool) -> Result<()> {
    sqlx::migrate!("./migrations")
        .set_ignore_missing(true)
        .run(pool)
        .await?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### 11. Testing Patterns

**SQLite Tests**:
- Setup: Create in-memory SQLite pool with `:memory:` URL
- Run migrations before each test: `crate::run_migrations(&pool).await.unwrap()`
- Initialize store: `SqliteCrmStore::new(pool)`
- Use explicit `.unwrap()` (allowed in tests via `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]`)

**Memory Tests**:
- Setup: `MemoryCrmStore::new()`
- No migrations needed
- Same test structure as SQLite

**Test Coverage**:
- CRUD operations (create, read, update via upsert, delete)
- External ID lookups
- Idempotent deletes
- Roundtrip serialization/deserialization
- PII field handling (encryption/redaction)
- Enum parsing and conversion
- Foreign key relationships (matter linked to contact, interaction linked to matter)

### 12. Naming Conventions

**Tables**: `crm_contacts`, `crm_matters`, `crm_interactions`, `crm_contact_channels`

**Indexes**: `idx_crm_contacts_updated_at`, `idx_crm_contact_channels_contact_id`, etc.

**Functions**: 
- List all: `list()`, `list_matters()`, `list_interactions_by_contact()`
- Get single: `get()`, `get_matter()`, `get_interaction()`
- Lookup by external: `get_by_external()`, `get_channel_by_external()`
- Write: `upsert()`, `upsert_matter()`, `upsert_channel()`
- Delete: `delete()`, `delete_matter()` (idempotent)

**Error Context**:
- Enum parsing: `Error::parse("contact.stage", value)` includes dot-separated path

### 13. Key Invariants & Patterns

1. **IDs are UUIDs v4** generated via `uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string()`
2. **Timestamps are milliseconds** since Unix epoch, stored as `u64` in code and `i64` in SQLite
3. **PII Protection**: Email and phone wrapped in `secrecy::Secret<String>`, exposed only at consumption points
4. **Enums stored as strings** in database, parsed with proper error context on retrieval
5. **Metadata is JSON** (arbitrary structure), serialized/deserialized as needed
6. **Foreign keys preserve referential integrity**: matters.contact_id uses `ON DELETE SET NULL`, interactions use `ON DELETE CASCADE`
7. **Unique constraints**: channel_type + channel_id must be unique across all contacts
8. **Ordering**: All list operations return results sorted by `updated_at DESC` (most recent first)
9. **Idempotent operations**: All delete operations succeed even if record doesn't exist
10. **No manual lock management**: Mutex only guards state, never used as a synchronization primitive (`Mutex<()>` forbidden)

### 14. Comparison with Other Store Implementations

| Aspect | Projects | Sessions | Cron | CRM |
|--------|----------|----------|------|-----|
| **Trait** | `ProjectStore` | `SessionStore` | `CronStore` | `CrmStore` |
| **SQLite Impl** | `SqliteProjectStore` | File-based | `SqliteStore` | `SqliteCrmStore` |
| **Memory Impl** | `TomlProjectStore` | File-based | `MemoryStore` | `MemoryCrmStore` |
| **Feature** | Always enabled | Always enabled | Optional | Optional (default enabled) |
| **Migrations** | Single file (609B) | Multiple files | Multiple files | Two files (6.3 KB total) |
| **Default Metrics** | No | Yes | No | No |
| **Error Type** | `thiserror::Error` | Custom | `anyhow::Result` | `thiserror::Error` |
| **Row Conversion** | `From<ProjectRow>` | N/A | `TryFrom` | `TryFrom` |
| **Constructor** | `new(pool)` | `new(sessions_dir)` | `new(pool)` | `new(pool)` |

This codebase demonstrates excellent patterns for:
- Type safety (enums, not strings)
- Security (PII wrapping, redacted debug)
- Testing (dual in-memory + SQLite implementations)
- Error handling (contextual parse errors)
- Schema evolution (ALTER TABLE strategy for SQLite)
- Async-first design (async_trait throughout)

   171→    async fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<Contact>> {

   182→    async fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>> {
   183→        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   184→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   185→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts WHERE id = ?",
   186→        )
   187→        .bind(id)
   188→        .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
   189→        .await?;
   190→
   191→        row.map(Contact::try_from).transpose()
   192→    }
   193→
   194→    async fn get_by_external(&self, source: &str, external_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Contact>> {
   195→        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, ContactRow>(
   196→            "SELECT id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, \
   197→             created_at, updated_at FROM crm_contacts WHERE source = ? AND external_id = ?",
   198→        )
   199→        .bind(source)
   200→        .bind(external_id)

   203→
   204→        row.map(Contact::try_from).transpose()
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    async fn upsert(&self, contact: Contact) -> Result<()> {
   208→        use secrecy::ExposeSecret;
   209→        let metadata = serde_json::to_string(&contact.metadata)?;
   210→        sqlx::query(
   211→            r#"INSERT INTO crm_contacts
   212→               (id, name, source, external_id, email, phone, stage, metadata, created_at, updated_at)
   213→               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   214→               ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET
   215→                 name        = excluded.name,
   216→                 source      = excluded.source,
   217→                 external_id = excluded.external_id,
   218→                 email       = excluded.email,
   219→                 phone       = excluded.phone,
   220→                 stage       = excluded.stage,
   221→                 metadata    = excluded.metadata,
   222→                 updated_at  = excluded.updated_at"#,
   223→        )
   224→        .bind(&contact.id)
   225→        .bind(&contact.name)
   226→        .bind(&contact.source)
   227→        .bind(&contact.external_id)
   228→        .bind(contact.email.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret().clone()))
   229→        .bind(contact.phone.as_ref().map(|s| s.expose_secret().clone()))
   230→        .bind(contact.stage.as_str())
   231→        .bind(&metadata)
   232→        .bind(contact.created_at as i64)
   233→        .bind(contact.updated_at as i64)
   234→        .execute(&self.pool)
   235→        .await?;
   236→        Ok(())
   237→    }

   239→    async fn delete(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()> {
   240→        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM crm_contacts WHERE id = ?")
   241→            .bind(id)
   242→            .execute(&self.pool)
   243→            .await?;
   244→        Ok(())
   245→    }

-- CRM tables
-- Owned by: moltis-crm crate

-- ── crm_contacts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contacts (
    id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    name        TEXT    NOT NULL,
    source      TEXT,
    external_id TEXT,
    metadata    TEXT,
    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,
    updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contacts_updated_at
    ON crm_contacts(updated_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contacts_source_external_id
    ON crm_contacts(source, external_id)
    WHERE source IS NOT NULL AND external_id IS NOT NULL;

-- ── crm_matters ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Matters represent cases, deals, or topics being tracked for a contact.
-- contact_id is nullable: a matter may not be tied to a single contact.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_matters (
    id         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    title      TEXT    NOT NULL,
    status     TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
    contact_id TEXT    REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    metadata   TEXT,
    created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_updated_at
    ON crm_matters(updated_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_contact_id
    ON crm_matters(contact_id)
    WHERE contact_id IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_matters_status
    ON crm_matters(status);

-- ── crm_interactions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Logged interactions (messages, calls, meetings, notes) with a contact.
-- contact_id is required; matter_id is optional.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_interactions (
    id         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    contact_id TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    matter_id  TEXT    REFERENCES crm_matters(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    kind       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    body       TEXT,
    metadata   TEXT,
    created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_contact_id
    ON crm_interactions(contact_id);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_matter_id
    ON crm_interactions(matter_id)
    WHERE matter_id IS NOT NULL;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_interactions_created_at
    ON crm_interactions(created_at DESC);

-- ── crm_contact_channels ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Maps contacts to communication channels (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack, …).
-- Each (channel_type, channel_identifier) pair belongs to exactly one contact.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_contact_channels (
    id                 TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
    contact_id         TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES crm_contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    channel_type       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    channel_identifier TEXT    NOT NULL,
    verified           INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE(channel_type, channel_identifier)
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_crm_contact_channels_contact_id
    ON crm_contact_channels(contact_id);
